name: Steel Sheds
description:
Steel sheds are bought on one question most sellers skip: not what the shed is for, but what steel it's made from and how long that steel will actually stand up on your site. SAMAN builds steel sheds as a factory-fabricated structure where the grade, the coating, and the sheet gauge are the specification you're paying for — the unit on this page is a 40 × 20 × 14 ft (800 sq ft) steel-and-wood build at ₹9,85,000 (regular ₹9,95,000), fabricated at our Bangalore and Greater Noida facilities and installed across India. If your decision comes down to material and durability rather than layout, this is the page for it.

What Actually Determines How Long a Steel Shed Lasts

A steel shed's lifespan is decided by three things, and the steel section itself is only one of them. The first is the coating system — bare or under-specified steel rusts; properly coated steel does not. The second is sheet and frame gauge — thinner sheet costs less today and dents, flexes, and corrodes through sooner. The third is maintenance access — an open or semi-open shed structure is exposed on more faces than an enclosed building, so the coating choice carries more weight.

A steel shed built for a 20-to-25-year structural life, shown on a prepared industrial plot.

Get all three right and a steel shed runs 20 to 25 years; under-spec any one and the same-looking shed is failing in five.

MS, Galvanised, or Pre-Galvanised — Matching the Steel to Your Site

"Steel" is three different specifications in a shed, and the right one is a site decision, not a price decision. A mild steel (MS) shed uses hot-rolled sections and colour-coated MS sheet — the most economical, correct for dry inland sites, and dependent on its factory coating for corrosion life. A galvanised steel shed uses hot-dip zinc-coated steel that protects itself even where scratched — the choice for coastal, high-humidity, or chemical-adjacent sites where you don't want to rely on paint alone. Pre-galvanised (Galvalume) sheet is a zinc-aluminium-coated cladding applied at the mill before fabrication — better corrosion resistance than colour-coated MS, used for wall and roof sheets rather than the frame.

Steel type Corrosion protection Cost vs MS Typical structural life
Mild Steel (MS) Factory paint coating Baseline 20–25 yrs (if coated & maintained)
Galvanised (hot-dip GI) Self-healing zinc layer +15–20% 25+ yrs, low maintenance
Pre-galvanised (Galvalume) Mill zinc-aluminium on sheet +8–12% 25–30 yrs on cladding

Galvanised steel resists corrosion on coastal and high-humidity sites where painted MS needs more upkeep.

If you're unsure, tell us the site location and humidity and we specify the type — MS inland, galvanised on the coast.

Is a "Tin Shed" the Same as a Steel Shed?

Not quite. A "tin shed" usually means a structure clad in thin galvanised sheet on a light frame — cheap and quick, but low-gauge and short-lived. A proper steel shed uses heavier structural sections and specified-gauge sheet engineered for a 20-to-25-year life. Same family, very different durability and cost.

A true structural steel shed uses heavier gauge and frame than a thin-sheet "tin shed."

The practical difference is gauge and frame. A thin-sheet tin shed flexes in wind and corrodes at the edges within a few years; a structural steel shed is sized to a load and a lifespan. When a quote looks unusually cheap, it is almost always a thinner gauge — worth confirming before you compare prices.

What the ₹9,85,000 / 800 sq ft Steel Shed Includes

The 40×20×14 ft, 800 sq ft steel shed delivered and assembled on a prepared site.

The configured unit on this page is 40 × 20 × 14 ft — 800 sq ft of covered area — in a steel-and-wood build at ₹9,85,000 (SKU SP-CC-40-2024). That price covers the structural steel frame, wall and roof sheeting, the wood infill elements, fabrication, delivery, and on-site assembly. Quoted separately is anything driven by your site or steel choice: an upgrade from MS to galvanised or Galvalume, a heavier sheet gauge for high-wind zones, added doors or openings, or a different footprint. If you need substantially more covered span than 800 sq ft, a clear-span warehouse structure is the format to price instead. Tell us the steel specification and site and the figure is exact, not a per-square-foot estimate.

Reading a Steel Shed Quote — Grade, Gauge, and the Mill Certificate

The fastest way to compare two steel shed quotes is to compare the steel, not the totals. Ask three things: the steel grade (IS 2062 for structural members is the Indian standard), the sheet gauge (a lower gauge number means thicker, longer-lasting sheet), and whether a mill test certificate is provided. SAMAN supplies the mill certificate for the structural steel in your order on request — it traces the material and is what a procurement team can actually verify.

Checking grade, gauge, and frame detail — what separates one steel shed quote from another.

A quote that won't name its grade or gauge is hiding where it saved money. For the full structural-grade and IS-certification detail across span widths, that sits on SAMAN's industrial shed range — this page stays on the material choice for a long-life steel shed.

Choosing by Material vs Choosing by Use — Where This Page Fits

This page is the right starting point when your decision is about the steel itself — grade, coating, gauge, and how many years it lasts. If the decision is really about what the shed does, start elsewhere in the range: for secure, enclosed holding of goods and stock, an enclosed goods-storage build is the better fit; for a showroom, retail outlet, or service bay that customers walk into, a customer-facing premises build is configured for that; and if the shed has to move between sites as your work shifts, look at a relocatable shed you can lift and re-site. Same steel engineering — the question is whether material or use is driving your choice.

Frequently Asked Questions

What are the disadvantages of a steel shed?

The honest ones are condensation and coating dependence. A sealed steel roof can sweat in temperature swings unless ventilated or insulated, and an under-coated steel shed will corrode where the coating fails. Both are solved at the specification stage — adequate gauge, the right coating for your climate, and ventilation where stored goods are moisture-sensitive. Specified correctly, neither is a problem over the structure's life.

What's the difference between an MS and a galvanised steel shed?

An MS shed relies on a factory paint coating to resist rust and needs that coating maintained over time. A galvanised shed has a hot-dip zinc layer bonded to the steel that protects it even if scratched, so it holds up better on coastal and high-humidity sites without repainting. Galvanised costs about 15–20% more and is the choice where corrosion risk is high.

How long does a steel shed last in India?

A correctly specified and maintained steel shed lasts 20 to 25 years, and a galvanised one longer. Lifespan is set by coating, sheet gauge, and maintenance — not by the steel section alone. The most common cause of early failure is an under-gauge sheet bought to hit a low price, which is why confirming gauge and coating matters more than the headline figure.

What does this steel shed cost?

The 40 × 20 × 14 ft (800 sq ft) configuration is ₹9,85,000, including frame, sheeting, wood infill, delivery, and assembly. Smaller or larger sheds, a galvanised upgrade, or a heavier gauge are quoted against your exact size and steel specification rather than a flat per-square-foot rate.

Get a Steel Shed Quote

Send your site location, climate, required size, and whether you want MS, galvanised, or Galvalume — we confirm the steel specification, an itemised price, and lead time within 24 hours.

Bangalore — Call +91 80886 85440 · WhatsApp +91 88616 22859
Delhi NCR — Call +91 87960 39938 · WhatsApp +91 97089 89937
